title: tetraneuris acaulis var. arizonica
intro: Tetraneuris acaulis var. arizonica, also known as Arizona pygmy marigold, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The medicinal properties of Tetraneuris acaulis var. arizonica include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and act as an expectorant to relieve coughs and congestion. Tetraneuris acaulis var. arizonica can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications such as salves and ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
